methods.method_name: Experimental Design for PlioCore and PICtrl
methods.description: The experimental design for PlioCore and associated PI control experiments involved model groups choosing between standard or enhanced PRISM4 boundary conditions, and deciding on vegetation treatment. The design aimed to address technical complexities in climate and earth system models.

steps.description: Model groups had a choice of which version of the PRISM4 boundary conditions to implement (standard or enhanced).
steps.searchable_summary: Select PRISM4 boundary conditions version for model implementation

steps.description: Model groups could either prescribe vegetation cover from the PRISM4 dataset or simulate the vegetation using a dynamic global vegetation model.
steps.searchable_summary: Decide on vegetation treatment method for model

steps.description: If simulating vegetation, models were required to be initialized with pre-industrial vegetation and spun-up until an equilibrium vegetation distribution is reached.
steps.searchable_summary: Initialize and spin-up model with pre-industrial vegetation

steps.description: The concentration of atmospheric CO2 for experiment PlioCore was set at 400 ppmv.
steps.searchable_summary: Set atmospheric CO2 concentration for PlioCore experiment

steps.description: Integration length was set to be 'as long as possible', or a minimum of 500 simulated years.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine integration length for model simulation

methods.description: Comparison of multi-model mean SST anomaly to proxy SST anomaly to assess model accuracy against observed data.

steps.description: Compare the multi-model mean SST anomaly to a proxy SST anomaly created by differencing the F&D19_30 data set from observed pre-industrial SSTs.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare multi-model mean SST anomaly to proxy SST anomaly

steps.description: Calculate the root mean square error (RMSE) between the model and the data.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate RMSE between model and data

steps.description: Assess model-data agreement by comparing proxy data ΔSST minus the mean ΔSST for individual models.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess model-data agreement using ΔSST comparison
